name: CIMA LL16: Embalse de Sallente Climb
name_original: Sube y baja Embalse de Sallente - Vall Fosca
description: Tackle the difficult 33.6-mile CIMA LL16: Embalse de Sallente Climb, gaining 4515 feet of elevation in Aigüestortes National Park.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Senterada are set within the dramatic landscapes of the Catalan Pyrenees, offering diverse natural features. The region is characterized by imposing peaks, narrow valleys, and the entrance to the picturesque Fosca Valley. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from open plains to high-altitude Pyrenean climbs, often on quiet, well-surfaced roads. Best road cycling routes around Senterada The most popular road cycling route is Lake Montcortès and Collegats Gorge Loop, a 36.1 miles (58.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and incorporates natural features like Lake Montcortès and the Congost de Collegats gorge. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is CIMA LL16: Embalse de Sallente Climb, a difficult 33.6 miles (54.0 km) path. This route involves a substantial climb within Aigüestortes National Park, featuring over 1,300 meters of elevation gain. Local road cyclists also love the The crown of Pallars, an 82.3 miles (132.4 km) trail leading through Pyrenean landscapes, often completed in about 7 hours 46 minutes. This very demanding route presents a significant challenge with over 2,600 meters of elevation gain. Road cycling around Senterada is defined by Pyrenean peaks, narrow valleys, and quiet, well-surfaced roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs to routes with varied gradients. The routes in Senterada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from several reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Senterada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Senterada?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes available around Senterada. These include 2 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 13 difficult routes, offering options for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Senterada?
A: Senterada offers a diverse range of terrain, from long, open plains to high-altitude Pyrenean climbs. You'll encounter narrow valleys, impressive peaks, and generally quiet, well-surfaced roads, making for a varied and immersive cycling experience.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Senterada?
A: Yes, Senterada offers 2 easy road cycling routes. While the region is known for its challenging climbs, there are options available for those new to road cycling or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Senterada?
A: Absolutely. Senterada is an excellent destination for experienced road cyclists, with 13 difficult routes. You can tackle significant climbs like the ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3387939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Triador&lt;/a&gt; or explore routes with substantial elevation gains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945301931/cima-ll16-embalse-de-sallente-climb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CIMA LL16: Embalse de Sallente Climb&lt;/a&gt; within Aigüestortes National Park.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes in Senterada?
A: Many routes pass by stunning natural features. You can cycle past &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortès&lt;/a&gt;, explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegats Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, or even spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2698547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Argenteria Cliffs and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region is rich in Pyrenean landscapes and picturesque valleys like the Fosca Valley.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Senterada?
A: Yes, you can find several circular road cycling routes. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944642311/lake-montcortes-and-collegats-gorge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortès and Collegats Gorge Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging ride through diverse landscapes and natural attractions.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Senterada?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning Pyrenean landscapes, the quiet and well-surfaced roads, and the variety of routes that cater to different fitness levels, from challenging climbs to scenic loops.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Senterada?
A: The region is excellent for outdoor activities, including road cycling, for much of the year. While specific conditions vary, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Autumn is particularly noted for mushroom picking in the lush forests, suggesting good conditions for cycling.

Q: Are there any specific mountain passes or climbs to look out for?
A: Yes, Senterada is surrounded by notable climbs. The ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3387939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Triador&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging climb. The wider Lleida Pyrenees also feature legendary passes like Port de la Bonaigua and Coll del Cantó, offering significant challenges for dedicated climbers.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other activities in Senterada?
A: Absolutely. Senterada is a hub for various mountain and rural tourism activities. Beyond cycling, you can enjoy hiking, trekking, mountain biking, 4x4 excursions, climbing, canyoning, and bird watching. The area also has cultural appeal with historical sites like the church of Santa Coloma de Bruguet.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for endurance training?
A: Yes, for endurance training, you can find very demanding routes. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1347466629/the-crown-of-pallars" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The crown of Pallars&lt;/a&gt;, an 82.3-mile (132.4 km) trail with over 2,600 meters of elevation gain, requiring very good fitness and offering a significant challenge.

In 2005 it had 85 inhabitants. It is a small charming town.

faqs.question: Is the CIMA LL16: Embalse de Sallente Climb suitable for road bikes?
faqs.answer: Despite being listed as a 'racebike' route, research indicates that the CIMA LL16: Embalse de Sallente Climb is likely not suitable for traditional road cycling. The path is described as an uphill, meandering route with a steep slope, crossing fields and an old railway line with tunnels. This terrain suggests unpaved or varied surfaces more appropriate for mountain biking or hiking.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this climb?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is varied and challenging. It features an uphill, meandering path with a quite steep slope. You can expect to cross areas of fields and an old railway line, known as the 'Carrilet,' which includes passing through tunnels. This suggests a mix of unpaved surfaces, making it more suitable for mountain bikes or sturdy trekking bikes rather than road bikes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse de Sallente climb?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts from the Sallente dam. You should look for parking options available near the Sallente Reservoir, which serves as a common starting point for routes in this area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle in the Embalse de Sallente area?
faqs.answer: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for snow at higher elevations, the best time of year to cycle this area is generally from late spring through early autumn (May to October). Always check the local weather forecast before heading out, especially for conditions around the Sallente Reservoir.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for cycling in Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, as this route is within the vicinity of Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, it's important to be aware of park regulations. While specific cycling permits for this exact route might not be required, always check the official park website for the latest information on access, fees, and any restrictions, especially concerning cycling in protected areas.

faqs.question: Is the CIMA LL16: Embalse de Sallente Climb dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information specific to dogs on this particular climb is limited. However, within Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, there are generally restrictions on where dogs are allowed, often requiring them to be on a leash and sometimes prohibiting them from certain areas to protect wildlife. It's best to consult the park's official guidelines regarding pets before planning your trip.

faqs.question: What scenic views or landmarks can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The area around the Sallente Reservoir and the path towards Estany Gento offers scenic natural landscapes. You can expect views of the reservoir itself, natural pools, and the surrounding mountainous environment. The route also passes through the settlement of Espui.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the CIMA LL16: Embalse de Sallente Climb intersects with several other trails. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the [CIMA LL16] Embalse de Sallente route (65%) and also includes sections of the Pedals de Foc (24%) and Ruta Transpirenaica en BTT (Noves de Segre - El Pont de Suert) (24%).

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this climb?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this route is approximately 3 hours and 14 minutes. However, due to the challenging and varied terrain, especially if you are not on a suitable bike, your actual time may vary. It's always wise to allow extra time for breaks and unexpected conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the Embalse de Sallente for cyclists?
faqs.answer: While specific cyclist-focused amenities are not detailed for the immediate trailhead, the nearby settlement of Espui may offer limited options for refreshments or a meal. For more choices, you might need to venture further into the Vall Fosca region.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this climb?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and varied terrain, you should bring a mountain bike or a robust trekking bike, appropriate safety gear including a helmet, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. A headlamp or bike light is also recommended if you plan to pass through the old railway tunnels.
